sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Merck is said to halt acquisition talks with cancer drug developer Revolution (MRK:NYSE)
feed.summary: Merck has reportedly ceased acquisition discussions with cancer drug developer Revolution Medicines due to a disagreement over valuation, according to The Wall Street Journal. Despite the halted talks, investor interest in Revolution Medicines remains strong, driven by upcoming clinical trial results for its pancreatic and colorectal cancer drugs. Mizuho Securities estimates that Revolution's pancreatic cancer drug could achieve $10 billion in annual global sales by 2035 if trials are successful.

feed.title: Linde stock price: what traders watch after a four-day run into Fed week
feed.summary: Linde (LIN) stock saw a 1.3% increase on Friday, extending its gains to a fourth consecutive session as the market anticipates the Federal Reserve's meeting and Linde's upcoming earnings report. The company also announced a significant board chair change set for January 31. Traders are closely monitoring economic indicators and the Fed's stance on inflation, which could impact industrial stocks like Linde, while investors await the Q4 2025 earnings report on February 5th for insights into future performance.

feed.title: Power outages reported throughout the Dominion service area
feed.summary: Dominion Power is reporting widespread power outages across its service area in Virginia and northeastern North Carolina due to a severe winter storm, with nearly 8,000 resources mobilized for restoration. Ice accumulation of over 0.25 inches is expected to cause extensive damage to power infrastructure and make restoration efforts challenging and multi-day, with ice road conditions and blocked roads impeding access. Customers are advised to report outages via the Dominion Energy mobile app or website, stay away from downed power lines, and prepare for extended restoration times.

feed.title: How Investors Are Reacting To RB Global (RBA) Earnings Beat, Dividend Hike And CEO Share Sale
feed.summary: RB Global (RBA) recently announced a quarterly cash dividend of US$0.31 per share and reported third-quarter 2025 earnings that surpassed analyst forecasts. This, alongside new contract wins and an option-related share sale by CEO James Francis Kessler, highlights the company's expanding market presence and its commitment to returning cash to shareholders. Despite its rising share price, the company could still be undervalued by 37%, though valuation and execution risks remain.

feed.title: Ameriprise (AMP) Joins CEO Coalition Urging Peace in Minnesota
feed.summary: Ameriprise Financial Inc. (AMP) has joined over 60 Minnesota-based CEOs in a coalition urging peace and collaboration to address community tensions in the state. The company, a significant financial services provider with strong wealth management and asset management franchises, demonstrates robust financial health with consistent growth and a high Piotroski F-Score. Despite some insider selling, Ameriprise maintains a compelling valuation and moderately positive analyst sentiment.

feed.title: Centerspace stock at a crossroads: Is the quiet Midwest landlord turning into a value opportunity?
feed.summary: Centerspace's stock is at a crossroads, with recent declines but a constructive one-year performance, leaving investors debating if the Midwestern apartment REIT is a value opportunity. The market is currently cautious due to interest rate jitters, with analysts generally holding a "Hold" rating but seeing potential for upside if rates fall and rent growth remains steady. Its future performance is tied to interest rate policy, operational efficiency in rent growth and expense control, and disciplined capital allocation.

feed.title: Verizon Communications Inc. (VZ) Earnings in Focus as Barclays Questions Telecom Competition Fears
feed.summary: Barclays has reinstated coverage of Verizon Communications Inc. (VZ) with an Equal Weight rating and a $43 price target, suggesting that fears of telecom competition might be overblown. Investors are eagerly awaiting Verizon's upcoming earnings report to gain insights into the industry's competitive landscape. The company recently issued a $20 credit to customers following a widespread 10-hour outage caused by a software problem, affecting various regions.

feed.title: Key Events to Monitor This Week: Fed Meeting, Apple, Meta, GM, Boeing, Chevron, and Others
feed.summary: This article highlights key events and market performance from the past week, noting significant market volatility. The S&P 500 experienced a 2.1% drop due to President Trump's tariff threats toward European countries but largely recovered after his softened stance at the World Economic Forum. The report also mentions upcoming events involving major corporations and the Federal Reserve meeting.

feed.title: McDonald’s stock (MCD) closed higher — what to watch before Wall Street reopens
feed.summary: McDonald’s shares closed Friday 1.05% higher at $309.25, with investor attention now turning to upcoming events including the launch of a limited-time Hot Honey Sauce on January 27, the Federal Reserve's FOMC meeting on January 27-28, and McDonald's earnings report slated for early February. Traders will be monitoring consumer spending trends, interest rate impacts, and new menu item performance as macro data and Fed decisions could significantly sway rate-sensitive consumer stocks.

feed.title: Merck Ends Talks to Acquire Revolution Medicines, WSJ Reports
feed.summary: Merck & Co. has reportedly ended discussions to acquire biotech firm Revolution Medicines Inc. due to a disagreement over price, according to the Wall Street Journal. The potential deal was estimated to be around $30 billion, and while talks have ceased, they could resume, or another suitor might emerge. Merck is actively seeking acquisitions to strengthen its treatment pipeline and offset anticipated sales losses from expiring patents.

feed.title: Edison International (EIX) in Focus as Morgan Stanley Refreshes its Utilities Outlook
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley recently updated its outlook on utilities, raising Edison International's (EIX) price target to $61 while maintaining an Underweight rating. The report comes after utilities underperformed the broader market in December. Edison International also announced its 22nd consecutive annual dividend increase, with the quarterly dividend reaching $0.8775 per share, reflecting management's confidence in the company's financial health and commitment to long-term EPS growth.

feed.title: Ann Arbor group to launch petition drive for new board in plan to dump DTE for city-owned electric utility
feed.summary: A group in Ann Arbor plans to launch a petition drive to establish a city-owned electric utility, aiming to replace DTE Energy due to concerns about fossil fuel reliance and service reliability. The group, Ann Arbor for Public Power, seeks to gather 6,500 signatures for a November ballot question to create a utility board, which would then consider buying DTE's infrastructure. DTE Energy argues that such a move could significantly increase rates and highlights its own efforts to improve reliability and support clean energy goals.

feed.title: Phillips 66 Wood River Refinery reports gas odor, says no danger
feed.summary: The Phillips 66 Wood River Refinery addressed gas odor concerns from residents, stating that while a minor leak occurred due to extreme cold, monitoring indicates no detectable levels of gas pose a risk, and the system has been repaired. This incident followed earlier flaring visible from the refinery, which company officials explained was a safety measure due to equipment tripping and assured the public of stable operations. Local fire departments shared the refinery's statement, advising residents experiencing symptoms to contact emergency services.

feed.title: Ford Motor Company (NYSE: F) Takes Nearly $20 Billion Charge to Revise EV Strategy
feed.summary: Ford Motor Company is taking a significant nearly $20 billion charge to recalibrate its electric vehicle strategy, which includes discontinuing the F-150 Lightning within four years. While not abandoning EVs, the company plans to introduce new models, such as a $30,000 midsize electric pickup by 2027, and reallocate investments towards more profitable hybrid and gasoline vehicles due to the slower-than-expected growth of the U.S. EV market. Wall Street analysts have adjusted price targets for Ford, with some raising them based on stronger global production and reduced EV losses.

feed.title: Fed Meeting, Apple, Meta, GM, Boeing, Chevron, and More to Watch This Week
feed.summary: The article previews a week of significant financial events including a Federal Reserve meeting, alongside earnings reports and updates from major companies like Apple, Meta, GM, Boeing, and Chevron. It notes the S&P 500's volatility last week, influenced by geopolitical events and presidential statements, before recovering most losses.

feed.title: Light & Wonder refinances US$2.13 billion term loan
feed.summary: Light & Wonder has refinanced a US$2.13 billion term loan by entering into an amendment to its 2022 credit agreement. This new tranche of term loans will replace existing debt and reduce the applicable margin to 2.00% or 1.0% using an alternate base rate, maturing on April 29, 2029. The original loan was secured to pay off high-interest legacy debt and support the company's gaming expansion following its demerger from Scientific Games.

feed.title: International Paper declares quarterly dividend
feed.summary: International Paper has announced its quarterly dividend for the period from January 1 to March 31, 2026. Common stock holders will receive 46.25 cents per share, while holders of the cumulative $4 preferred stock will receive $1 per share. Both dividends are payable on March 17, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 23, 2026.

feed.title: Hotel101 eyes $300-M US fundraising in the US
feed.summary: Hotel101 Global Holdings Corp., a homegrown hotel chain developer, plans to raise up to $300 million through the issuance of perpetual convertible preferred shares in the United States. This fundraising, approved by parent company DoubleDragon Corp., aims to accelerate the global rollout of Hotel101's standardized projects and strengthen DoubleDragon's balance sheet. The company targets expansion into 100 countries with its "HappyRoom" model and expects its first global project, Hotel101-Madrid, to open by March 2026.

feed.title: Severe winter storm mobilizes 8,000 Dominion Energy resources across our region
feed.summary: Dominion Energy has mobilized nearly 8,000 resources, including contractors and support personnel, in response to a severe winter storm bringing ice and dangerous conditions to Virginia and northeastern North Carolina. The storm is expected to cause widespread power outages, with around 13,000 customers already affected. Dominion Energy warns customers to prepare for a multi-day restoration effort, prioritizing critical services and advising caution regarding downed power lines.

feed.title: IYK vs. PBJ: Blue-Chip Stability or Concentrated Food Bets?
feed.summary: This article compares two ETFs, Invesco Food & Beverage ETF (PBJ) and iShares US Consumer Staples ETF (IYK), highlighting their differences in cost, dividend yield, and sector breadth. IYK offers broader exposure to consumer staples, including healthcare, a lower expense ratio, and higher dividend yield, while PBJ is more concentrated in food and beverage companies. IYK has shown stronger recent performance, suggesting it might be a more stable choice for defensive investing, whereas PBJ is suited for investors with specific conviction in the food and beverage sector.

feed.title: Regulators to handle challenges to CWIP requests by OGE
feed.summary: The Oklahoma Corporation Commission is set to address challenges to Oklahoma Gas and Electric's (OGE) Construction Work in Progress (CWIP) requests for its Horseshoe Lake Power Plant expansion. OGE faces opposition from consumer groups and the Commission's Public Utilities Division, who argue that OGE is attempting to bypass foundational requirements for CWIP recovery. The dispute centers on a new law, SB998, which allows CWIP for natural gas projects but which opponents contend OGE is applying retroactively and incorrectly, especially since a previous pre-approval order rejected OGE's CWIP request.

feed.title: Kroger and Walmart remain open in Houston winter storm
feed.summary: Kroger and Walmart are maintaining operations in Houston during a winter storm, while H-E-B has modified its hours, opening later than usual in the Houston area and implementing stricter limitations in East Texas. All retailers emphasize customer safety and advise checking store-specific updates, with Walmart offering an interactive store locator. Customers are urged to consider essential travel only due to hazardous road conditions.

feed.title: How Investors May Respond To H&R Block (HRB) Adding Consumer and Finance Veterans to Its Board
feed.summary: H&R Block recently appointed Geralyn R. Breig, Christian H. Charnaux, and Stephanie C. Plaines to its Board of Directors, bringing expertise in consumer brands, expansion, and corporate finance. This move aims to enhance client experience, growth, and omnichannel capabilities, reinforcing the company's investment narrative as a cash-generative tax franchise with a reliable dividend. Investors will be watching how these appointments impact capital allocation and omnichannel execution, especially given the company's existing debt load and negative equity.

feed.title: TechCrunch Mobility: RIP, Tesla Autopilot, and the NTSB investigates Waymo
feed.summary: Tesla has made significant moves in its automated driving technology, including offering robotaxi rides in Austin without a human safety driver and discontinuing its standard Autopilot system. This aligns with Tesla's strategy to generate more revenue from its Full Self-Driving software and position itself as an AI and robotics company. Meanwhile, the NTSB is investigating Waymo over reports of its robotaxis illegally passing stopped school buses.

feed.title: Illinois Tool Works (NYSE:ITW) Has A Pretty Healthy Balance Sheet
feed.summary: Illinois Tool Works (NYSE:ITW) carries a moderate amount of debt, with net debt at US$8.02 billion as of September 2025. Despite its liabilities outweighing immediate assets by US$8.75 billion, the company's strong market capitalization of US$74.9 billion and impressive interest coverage indicate a healthy balance sheet. The ability to generate robust free cash flow, equating to 67% of its EBIT, further strengthens its position to manage and potentially reduce debt.

feed.title: Assessing Brinker International (EAT) Valuation After Analyst Upgrades And Earnings Surprises
feed.summary: Brinker International (EAT) is gaining investor attention following analyst upgrades, improved perceptions of Chili's, and strong earnings surprises. The stock has seen significant share price momentum, and its fair value is estimated at $175.71, suggesting it is currently undervalued at $162.77 per share. This valuation relies on projected 4.7% annual revenue growth over the next three years and operational efficiency improvements.

feed.title: Assessing ICU Medical (ICUI) Valuation As Growth Slows And Sales Are Forecast To Decline
feed.summary: ICU Medical (ICUI) is under investor scrutiny due to projected slower revenue growth and an estimated 7.7% sales decline over the next year, contrasting with a 24.2% share price increase over the last three months. Despite these concerns, its valuation narrative suggests it is 15.6% undervalued, with a fair value pegged at $180.83 per share due to its innovation pipeline and potential for increased recurring revenues. However, this valuation depends on manageable tariffs and regulatory scrutiny.

feed.title: A Look At Global Net Lease (GNL) Valuation After Asset Sales And Leverage Reduction Efforts
feed.summary: Global Net Lease (GNL) has been actively selling assets, including the McLaren Campus, to reduce leverage and reposition its portfolio. While the market narrative suggests GNL is 1% overvalued at $9.40 compared to a fair value of $9.36, a discounted cash flow (DCF) model indicates a fair value of $14.64, suggesting a heavy market discount on future cash flows. Investors are encouraged to consider these different valuation perspectives and potential risks associated with office exposure and reliance on asset sales.

feed.title: Assessing DaVita (DVA) Valuation As Earnings Growth Optimism Builds Ahead Of Quarterly Results
feed.summary: Ahead of its Q4 earnings release, DaVita (DVA) is drawing investor attention due to expected earnings per share growth and its current discount compared to healthcare peers. Despite mixed recent share price performance, optimism is building around its valuation, with Simply Wall St's narrative suggesting a fair value of $143, indicating the stock is undervalued. This valuation is based on sustained demand for dialysis from an aging population and cost reductions driven by technology investments, though risks like persistent volume pressure or rising costs could challenge these assumptions.

feed.title: Infosys caps WFO exemption at 5 days a quarter after Wipro & TCS tighten WFH norms
feed.summary: Infosys has tightened its work-from-home (WFH) policy, limiting exemptions to five days per quarter for employees seeking additional WFH days, with exceptions only for critical medical conditions. This move follows similar policy tightening by competitors like TCS and Wipro, as IT companies push for more in-office collaboration amid evolving project timelines and muted revenue growth.

feed.title: Chevron stock (CVX) set for Monday focus after Venezuela talks; Tengiz outage lingers
feed.summary: Chevron (CVX) is expected to be a focal point for investors on Monday due to ongoing U.S. discussions about increasing Venezuela's crude output with Chevron and other companies, which could significantly boost production. This development comes as oil prices surged amid Middle East supply concerns. Concurrently, Chevron is dealing with a lingering outage at Kazakhstan's Tengiz field, impacting its oil production and adding complexity to its operational outlook ahead of its fourth-quarter earnings release.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges F5, Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against F5, Inc. (NASDAQ: FFIV) on behalf of investors who purchased its securities between October 28, 2024, and October 27, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that F5 made false and misleading statements about its security capabilities and concealed a significant security breach, leading to artificially inflated stock prices. Investors are encouraged to join the case, with a lead plaintiff deadline of February 17, 2026.

feed.title: Do Strong Q3 Results And New Preferred Equity Program Change The Bull Case For National Storage Affiliates Trust (NSA)?
feed.summary: National Storage Affiliates Trust (NSA) reported strong Q3 2025 results with improved revenue growth and FFO per share exceeding expectations. The company also launched a new preferred equity program to fund self-storage deals, aiming to add fee and income streams without significant balance sheet strain. Investors are now focused on upcoming Q4 2025 results and 2026 guidance to assess whether these positive trends can sustain the company's investment narrative amidst mixed analyst sentiment and a premium valuation.

feed.title: The Truth About Northern Trust Corp.: Quiet Stock, Loud Money Moves
feed.summary: Northern Trust Corp. (NTRS) is presented as a stable, long-term investment for wealth preservation, contrasting with volatile meme stocks. The article highlights its focus on wealth management, asset servicing, and institutional investing, offering stability and regular dividends. It suggests Northern Trust is suited for investors seeking a low-drama, income-generating component in a diversified portfolio rather than explosive growth.

feed.title: Prudential Financial, Inc. (PRU) in Focus as Wells Fargo Outlines Key Themes Across Insurance
feed.summary: Wells Fargo analyst Elyse Greenspan raised the price target for Prudential Financial, Inc. (PRU) to $115 from $113, maintaining an Equal Weight rating, while outlining key focus areas for the insurance sector, including pricing trends for P&C insurers, organic growth for brokers, and sales and capital for life insurers. Separately, the CEO of Prudential Financial’s Japan unit is set to resign following revelations of widespread employee misconduct totaling approximately 3.1 billion yen ($19.60 million) involving improper solicitations and embezzlement that affected 498 customers. The company continues its review of similar cases of misconduct first flagged in 2024.

feed.title: Precision Trading with Privia Health Group Inc. (PRVA) Risk Zones
feed.summary: This article provides a precision trading analysis for Privia Health Group Inc. (PRVA), highlighting a strong near-term sentiment with neutral mid and long-term outlooks. It details three trading strategies—Position, Momentum Breakout, and Risk Hedging—along with their respective entry, target, and stop-loss zones. The analysis emphasizes an exceptional 31.4:1 risk-reward setup targeting a 9.4% gain versus 0.3% risk.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges F5, Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against F5, Inc. (NASDAQ: FFIV) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between October 28, 2024, and October 27, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that F5 made false and misleading statements about its security capabilities, concealing a significant security breach that would impair its market opportunities. Investors who suffered losses are encouraged to join the lawsuit, with a lead plaintiff deadline of February 17, 2026.

feed.title: Eaton named as top 2026 pick at BNP Paribas on data center demand (ETN:NYSE)
feed.summary: BNP Paribas Equity Research has designated Eaton (ETN) as a top investment pick for 2026, citing significant demand acceleration from AI data centers. The firm believes there is a disconnect between Eaton's strong fundamentals and its current valuation, presenting a buying opportunity. Additionally, BNP Paribas expects Eaton's margins and earnings to improve as capacity utilization increases and AI-driven data center revenues grow.

feed.title: Fortinet Stock Surges on TD Cowen Upgrade to Buy
feed.summary: Fortinet (FTNT) stock surged over 5% following an upgrade to 'Buy' by TD Cowen, with a price target of $100, driven by anticipated growth in the cybersecurity market and the company's limited exposure to China. The company demonstrates strong profitability with an EBIT margin of 34.7% and robust revenue growth, positioning it for continued market leadership despite potential balance sheet constraints. Analysts highlight favorable valuation and positive outlooks for fiscal 2026, reinforced by Fortinet's strategic global collaborations.

feed.title: Major new amusement park coming to DisneyWorld’s backyard
feed.summary: Mattel Inc. is planning to build its first of five indoor water parks in Orlando, near DisneyWorld. The parks will feature Mattel's popular brands like Barbie and Hot Wheels in various water-based attractions. This development marks a new move for Mattel into experiential entertainment, in partnership with Martin Aquatic and American Resort Management.

feed.title: AI’s Payroll Power Play: ISG Ranks Leaders Reshaping Employee Value
feed.summary: ISG's 2025 Buyers Guides recognize ADP, Oracle, and UKG as top payroll leaders, emphasizing AI's role in improving error detection, compliance, and employee financial solutions. The report predicts that by 2028, half of all companies will use AI to proactively address payroll issues, enhancing business resilience. This shift highlights payroll software as a strategic tool for employee value and business stability, with AI and machine learning driving real-time visibility and regulatory adaptability.

feed.title: A Look At Intapp (INTA) Valuation After Monarch Partnership Expansion And DealCloud Reseller Agreement
feed.summary: Intapp (INTA) is gaining investor attention following Monarch Financial Technology Solutions' expanded partnership to resell DealCloud and support private capital market clients, ahead of its February earnings webcast. Despite recent share price declines, Intapp is considered undervalued by analysts, with a most followed narrative placing its fair value at $57.88, driven by expected revenue growth from AI capabilities and cloud adoption. However, risks like integration challenges and market valuation tension suggest caution for investors.

feed.title: Shareholders Should Be Pleased With McKesson Corporation's (NYSE:MCK) Price
feed.summary: McKesson Corporation (NYSE:MCK) currently has a high P/E ratio of 25.1x, which is above the industry average. However, this premium is justified by its strong recent earnings growth of 66% and anticipated future EPS growth of 17% per annum. Analysts believe this robust outlook makes shareholders unwilling to sell, supporting the current valuation.

feed.title: What You Can Learn From Generac Holdings Inc.'s (NYSE:GNRC) P/E
feed.summary: Generac Holdings Inc. (NYSE:GNRC) currently has a high P/E ratio of 32.5x, which is significantly above the market average in the United States and may suggest bearish signals. However, this high P/E is attributed to analyst forecasts predicting strong future earnings growth for Generac, at 23% per annum, compared to the market's 12%. Investors appear willing to pay a premium for this anticipated superior growth, making the high P/E justified for now.

feed.title: CenterPoint: More than 99% of Houston-area customers have power
feed.summary: CenterPoint Energy announced that over 99% of its Houston-area customers have had their power restored following a recent winter storm. Despite the widespread restoration, the company emphasized that any outage is a priority and has mobilized 3,300 workers to continue damage assessments and ensure all service is back online. Customers can use CenterPoint's Outage Tracker for real-time updates.

feed.title: Fortinet Stock in Focus: Can a Cybersecurity Veteran Still Outperform after a Rocky Year?
feed.summary: Fortinet (FTNT) stock has experienced a volatile year, with negative trailing twelve-month performance, but Wall Street is showing renewed optimism. While short-term challenges exist, including cautious customer spending on network hardware and competitive pressures, analysts anticipate potential upside driven by Fortinet's platform vision, AI integration, and operational discipline. The current consolidation phase could be an opportunity for patient investors or a signal to await clearer earnings signals.

feed.title: Delta operates reduced schedule amid ongoing winter weather
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines is operating a reduced flight schedule due to ongoing winter weather affecting several U.S. regions, including the Northeast and its Atlanta hub. The airline has made proactive schedule adjustments and canceled additional flights to compensate for the significant disruptions caused by ice, snow, and strong winds across the South, East, and central parts of the U.S. The National Weather Service warns of hazardous travel conditions and potential power outages across the Southeast as the storm progresses.

feed.title: Luck or Strategy? Why Boeing uses "7" in all its aircraft names
feed.summary: Boeing's iconic use of the number "7" in its aircraft names, such as the 707 and 787, originated from an internal numbering system in the 1950s where the 700 series was designated for commercial jet transports. The success of the Boeing 707 solidified this "7X7" pattern as a brand, making it instantly recognizable globally. While the company is nearing the end of available "7X7" combinations with only the 797 remaining for traditional designs, Boeing is exploring new naming conventions like 7A7-7Z7 or leveraging letters for variants to accommodate future revolutionary aircraft designs.

feed.title: See where winter storm power outages in Georgia are located
feed.summary: A massive winter storm has caused significant power outages across Georgia due to ice accumulation on trees and power lines. Georgia Power and electric cooperatives are warning customers to prepare for extended outages, as dangerous conditions may hinder restoration efforts. An interactive outage map from The Atlanta Journal-Constitution is available to show the concentration of outages and affected electricity providers.

feed.title: Hecla Mining (HL) Leads with 29.31% Weekly Gain Amid Precious Metals Surge
feed.summary: Hecla Mining (HL) experienced a 29.31% weekly gain, leading a surge in precious metals stocks due to geopolitical tensions, a weakening US dollar, and expectations of a Fed rate cut. First Majestic Silver Corp. (AG) also saw a significant rise of 26.57%. The article also highlights positive developments for Venture Global, Inc. and Moderna, Inc.
